from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase (more concise for common predicates)
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "was started in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "rises to",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is in",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "leader is",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "postal code is",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to avoid repeating the subject in every clause.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    # Helper to clean quoted objects
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        return o.strip('"').strip("'")

    # Predicates that typically describe an actor (person/organisation) acting on a building
    actor_predicates = {"architect", "owner", "developer", "tenant", "significantBuilding", "significantProject"}

    # Build sentences, trying to merge actor information when possible
    sentences = []
    processed = set()

    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        if subject in processed:
            continue

        # Look for a triple where the object is also a subject (actor relationship)
        merged = False
        for tr in group:
            if tr.predicate in actor_predicates and tr.object in subject_groups:
                actor = tr.object
                actor_triples = subject_groups[actor]

                # Build descriptor clauses for the actor (exclude the linking predicate to avoid repetition)
                actor_parts = []
                for a_tr in actor_triples:
                    if a_tr.predicate == "birthPlace":
                        actor_parts.append(f"was born in {clean_obj(a_tr.object)}")
                    elif a_tr.predicate == "deathPlace":
                        actor_parts.append(f"died in {clean_obj(a_tr.object)}")
                    elif a_tr.predicate == "significantBuilding":
                        actor_parts.append(f"is known for the building {clean_obj(a_tr.object)}")
                    elif a_tr.predicate == "significantProject":
                        actor_parts.append(f"is known for the project {clean_obj(a_tr.object)}")
                    elif a_tr.predicate == "architecturalStyle":
                        actor_parts.append(f"has an architectural style of {clean_obj(a_tr.object)}")
                    # add more simple descriptors as needed
                # Join actor descriptors with commas
                actor_desc = ""
                if actor_parts:
                    actor_desc = ", who " + ", ".join(actor_parts)

                # Verb phrase for the linking predicate
                verb = verb_map.get(tr.predicate, "has")
                # Build the main clause
                main_clause = f"{actor}{actor_desc} {verb} {subject}"
                sentences.append(main_clause + ".")
                processed.add(subject)
                processed.add(actor)
                merged = True
                break

        if merged:
            continue

        # Fallback: build a simple sentence for the subject
        parts = []
        handled = set()
        for tr in group:
            # Simple possessive handling
            if tr.predicate in {"capital", "language", "birthPlace", "deathPlace", "leaderTitle", "demonym"}:
                phrase = tr.predicate
                parts.append(f"The {phrase} of {subject} is {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
            elif tr.predicate == "buildingStartDate":
                parts.append(f"Construction of {subject} began in {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
            elif tr.predicate == "inaugurationDate":
                parts.append(f"The inauguration of {subject} was on {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
            elif tr.predicate == "height":
                raw_val = clean_obj(tr.object)
                val = raw_val.split('(')[0].strip()
                parts.append(f"The height of {subject} is {val} metres")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
            elif tr.predicate == "leader":
                parts.append(f"{clean_obj(tr.object)} is the leader of {subject}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)

        # Preferred ordering
        order = ["location", "height", "architect", "buildingType", "completionDate"]
        for pred in order:
            if pred in handled:
                continue
            for tr in group:
                if tr.predicate == pred:
                    verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
                    parts.append(f"{verb} {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                    handled.add(pred)
                    break

        # Remaining predicates
        for tr in group:
            if tr.predicate in handled:
                continue
            verb = verb_map.get(tr.predicate, "has")
            obj = clean_obj(tr.object)

            if tr.predicate == "floorCount":
                clause = f"{verb} {obj} floors"
            elif tr.predicate == "bedCount":
                clause = f"{verb} {obj} beds"
            elif tr.predicate == "numberOfRooms":
                clause = f"{verb} {obj} rooms"
            elif tr.predicate == "country":
                if any(keyword in obj for keyword in ["States", "Kingdom", "Republic", "Union", "Islands", "Federated"]):
                    clause = f"{verb} the {obj}"
                else:
                    clause = f"{verb} {obj}"
            else:
                clause = f"{verb} {obj}"
            parts.append(clause)

        # Assemble sentence
        if parts:
            # If there is only one clause and it already forms a complete sentence
            # (e.g., "Sumatra Mahajan is the leader of India"), use it directly.
            if len(parts) == 1:
                sentence = parts[0].strip()
                # Ensure it ends with a period.
                if not sentence.endswith('.'):
                    sentence = sentence + '.'
                # Capitalize first character.
                sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts) + "."
                sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
        else:
            sentence = f"{subject}."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
    if final_text:
        final_text = final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
